title: How did the workers of Tokyo 3 rebuild so fast
display_title: How did the workers of Tokyo 3 rebuild so fast
wikitext:
[]: How did the workers of Tokyo 3 rebuild so fast?             Quote Post by BeoX2 » Wed Sep 01, 2010 7:18 pm Throughout many parts of the series, large amounts of buildings, sections of NERV hq. and especially the Evas are massively damaged, yet it seems that all of it is easily rebuilt within a week. How can this be possible, even with "all of the world's resources being poured into it." For instance, when Zeruel attacks and destroys all of the underground bulkheads above NERV HQ., they seem to have them totally rebuilt in time for the next Angel to attack. The only time that they never rebuild is after Ep. 23 (for obvious reasons). Perhaps, as Ritsuko mentioned, they utilized the "particle-wave matter" to build things quickly, or perhaps they used the Evas. Who knows.

[]:             Quote Post by Carling » Wed Sep 01, 2010 7:28 pm My guess is that NERV has a load of money, or there are lots of back up buildings?

[]:             Quote Post by BeoX2 » Wed Sep 01, 2010 7:29 pm I know, but just the consumption of time. You can't raise a building in a week.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Wed Sep 01, 2010 7:44 pm It's very unclear how much time goes by between the episodes, and on rare occasion are we given any kind of time frame. In Ramiel's case, we see workers tearing down it's body for a few more episodes after it's death, so we see that Tokyo-3 isn't repaired by super men.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Thu Sep 02, 2010 1:05 am We all know that the Japanese have extensive experience with massive urban reconstruction after having to deal with Godzillla and all the rest on an on-going basis.

[]:             Quote Post by AyrYntake » Thu Sep 02, 2010 1:47 am You're thinking too far into it. The camera crew just pick a different spot of Tokyo-3 to film every time. As for the underground bulkheads, as you call them, there wasn't a single Angel after Zeruel to forcefully break through into the Geo Front...

[]:             Quote Post by InstrumentalityOne » Thu Sep 02, 2010 5:09 am We don´t even know how much time has passed since episode 1 and episode 24, it´s really really shady how much time passed between the angels.

[]:             Quote Post by BeoX2 » Thu Sep 02, 2010 6:02 am InstrumentalityOne wrote: We don´t even know how much time has passed since episode 1 and episode 24, it´s really really shady how much time passed between the angels.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Thu Sep 02, 2010 8:13 am BeoX2 wrote: But I think it's safe to say the series couldn't have taken place in more than a year.

[]:             Quote Post by soul.assassin » Thu Sep 02, 2010 8:16 am To answer the question, perhaps these might suffice 1.) In Rebuild, NERV has a fairly large construction and public works division, complete with heavy equipment and even huge excavators, and with that much manpower and resources, some projects can be completed anywhere from two months to less than a week. The preparations for Operation Yashima is one example. 2.) As there are prefab homes now available in Japan IRL, it's not a problem assembling prefab buildings to replace those damaged in battle. 3.) Buildings that have suffered damage can be repaired while retracted underground.

[]:             Quote Post by Sharaz Destler » Thu Sep 02, 2010 12:18 pm Mr. Tines wrote: We all know that the Japanese have extensive experience with massive urban reconstruction after having to deal with Godzillla and all the rest on an on-going basis.

[]:             Quote Post by Baz » Thu Sep 02, 2010 12:34 pm Sharaz Destler wrote: There's also something I read about last night called "transparent aluminum" that is often used in place of glass. Completely shatterproof, or so I heard.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Thu Sep 02, 2010 12:35 pm Science beams.

[]:             Quote Post by InstrumentalityOne » Thu Sep 02, 2010 12:39 pm Baz wrote: Was this in a science-fictiion context or a real-world context? E.g. sapphire (Al2O3) could be called transparent aluminum, but it is far too expensive to use for anything but watch faces and jet-fighter windows. And then there is the transparent aluminum from Star Trek IV, which was completely different. I like the "modularization" theories presented in this thread. Need new buildings? Have the government "requisition" them from some other Japanese city.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Thu Sep 02, 2010 2:57 pm InstrumentalityOne wrote: NERV has unlimited money.

[]:             Quote Post by tomrule123 » Thu Sep 02, 2010 6:17 pm AyrYntake wrote: You're thinking too far into it. The camera crew just pick a different spot of Tokyo-3 to film every time.

[]:             Quote Post by lesmiserables » Fri Sep 03, 2010 12:35 am tomrule123 wrote: ... you'll need probably 2000 workers to rebuild that fast.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Fri Sep 03, 2010 6:34 am I'd assume most of Tokyo-3 is prefabricated and that, due to second impact, there is actually a constant demand for such structures in reconstruction efforts worldwide. Nerv getting first dibs while other cities are left half-finished isn't really a stretch of imagination.

[]:             Quote Post by InstrumentalityOne » Fri Sep 03, 2010 7:25 am Ornette wrote: ...

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Fri Sep 03, 2010 9:02 am I don't think it's a matter of rebuilding so much as it is clearing debris. But even then, it does take them some time. In most cases, they just fill the hole and create a new lake. They even abandon the command center after Zeruel attacks. Also, cartoonishness.

[]: How did the workers of Tokyo 3 rebuild so fast?             Quote Post by BeoX2 » Wed Sep 01, 2010 7:18 pm Throughout many parts of the series, large amounts of buildings, sections of NERV hq. and especially the Evas are massively damaged, yet it seems that all of it is easily rebuilt within a week. How can this be possible, even with "all of the world's resources being poured into it." For instance, when Zeruel attacks and destroys all of the underground bulkheads above NERV HQ., they seem to have them totally rebuilt in time for the next Angel to attack. The only time that they never rebuild is after Ep. 23 (for obvious reasons). Perhaps, as Ritsuko mentioned, they utilized the "particle-wave matter" to build things quickly, or perhaps they used the Evas. Who knows.
